text.title: Axum AppError enum: thiserror, IntoResponse, public message scrubbing
text.summary: AppError enum using thiserror for display, implementing IntoResponse to map variants to HTTP status codes and safe JSON bodies, with internal errors scrubbed before reaching the client.

section.title: AppError enum

rule.statement: Define AppError as a thiserror enum with one variant per error class. Use #[from] derives for sqlx::Error, validator::ValidationErrors, reqwest::Error, and anyhow::Error so ? works everywhere. Implement IntoResponse to map variants to (StatusCode, Json) tuples.
rule.rationale: thiserror generates Display automatically from the #[error("...")] attribute, eliminating manual fmt::Display implementations. #[from] derives let ? propagate library errors into AppError without explicit map_err calls.

section.reference: Full AppError enum with IntoResponse implementation.

example.code: #[derive(Debug, Error)] pub enum AppError { #[error("not found")] NotFound, #[error("unauthorized")] Unauthorized, #[error("validation: {0}")] Validation(#[from] validator::ValidationErrors), #[error("conflict: {0}")] Conflict(String), #[error("database: {0}")] Database(#[from] sqlx::Error), #[error("internal: {0}")] Internal(#[from] anyhow::Error), }

rule.statement: In IntoResponse, map Database, Upstream, and Internal variants to "internal error" in the public message, regardless of the internal error text. Only NotFound, Unauthorized, Conflict, and Validation messages are client-safe.
rule.rationale: Database error text contains table names, column names, constraint names, and sometimes query fragments — all sensitive. The internal error is already captured by tracing::error! for server-side correlation.
